name: hybrid-classify-inbox
description: RFC 0011 hybrid-serve reference consumer: classifies a batch of inbox messages by consulting a distilled model first (talos:core/model predict-batch, lifecycle-gated server-side) and routing ONLY the messages the model isn't confident about to the LLM. Emits the same {classifications:[{idx,bucket}]} shape a plain LLM classify node does — so a downstream organize node is unchanged — plus an __ml_distill__ envelope for the LLM-labeled subset only (active learning; the model's own predictions are never distilled back). While the model is in shadow/llm_only the gate makes it abstain on everything, so behavior is identical to the pre-hybrid organizer; the LLM-cost reduction turns on automatically when the model earns hybrid. Both the model's and the LLM's labels are validated against the allowed bucket set before they reach the output.

MODEL_NAME.description: Registered ml_models name to consult (e.g. 'inbox-classifier-personal'). Resolved under the execution user's tenancy; served only once the model reaches hybrid/fast_primary (shadow → abstain-all → LLM handles everything).

SYSTEM_PROMPT.description: The classification instructions for the LLM leg. Must instruct the model to return {"classifications":[{"idx":<int>,"bucket":"follow_up|to_read|archive"}]} referencing each message by its idx. Email content is wrapped in <untrusted_data> (spotlighting) before the LLM sees it.

MODEL.description: LLM model identifier for the fallback leg.

PROVIDER.description: LLM provider for the fallback leg. 'ollama' (default, Tier 1, local) keeps email content on-host.

MAX_TOKENS.description: Max tokens for the LLM classification response.
